title: Fail fast on unparseable LLM output; fallback to honest deterministic draft
context: The parser was returning hollow/useless LLM drafts (marked "llm_draft") when the model output was truncated, refused, or prose-only. Silent degradation eroded reliability and falsely signaled confidence in corrupted records.
decision: Redesigned to throw on failed JSON extraction, triggering safe wrappers (draftDecisionSafe/draftBugSafe) to return deterministic fallback drafts explicitly labeled "inferred" with low confidence. Also hardened the parser itself (string-aware balancing, content-based selection, explicit type coercion) to reduce false positives.
consequences: Output honesty: users see "inferred" for determinstic fallbacks, "llm_draft" only when real JSON was extracted
Deterministic drafts (diff-structured) are more useful than truncated/corrupted LLM attempts
Error cases are explicit, not silent
Test coverage grown from 47 to 76 (each verified repro encoded)
alternatives_rejected: Keep returning hollow drafts silently — loses honesty and reliability signal
Return best-effort partial JSON parsing — corrupts downstream logic
Retry the LLM — doesn't fix truncation and adds latency
Position-based selection — can't distinguish real answer from template/recap
